134	      PyErr_SetString(PyExc_RuntimeError, err);
135	      PyGILState_Release(gil_state);
136	   }
137	}
138	
139	#define HIP_CHECK(ans) gpuAssert((ans), __FILE__, __LINE__);
140	
141	static void ensureHipContext() {
142	  hipDevice_t device;
143	  HIP_CHECK(hipGetDevice(&device));
144	  // HIP context is created automatically when needed
145	}
146	
147	
148	typedef struct _DevicePtrInfo {
149	    hipDeviceptr_t dev_ptr;
150	    bool valid;
151	} DevicePtrInfo;
152	
153	static PyObject* data_ptr_str = NULL;
154	
155	static inline DevicePtrInfo getPointer(PyObject *obj, int idx) {
156	  DevicePtrInfo ptr_info;
157	  ptr_info.dev_ptr = 0;
158	  ptr_info.valid = true;
159	  if (PyLong_Check(obj)) {
160	    ptr_info.dev_ptr = (hipDeviceptr_t)PyLong_AsUnsignedLongLong(obj);
161	    return ptr_info;
162	  }
163	  if (obj == Py_None) {
164	    // valid nullptr
165	    return ptr_info;
166	  }
167	  PyObject *ret = PyObject_CallMethodNoArgs(obj, data_ptr_str);
168	  if (!ret) {
169	    PyErr_SetString(PyExc_TypeError, "Pointer argument must be either uint64 or have data_ptr method");
170	    ptr_info.valid = false;
171	    goto cleanup;
172	  }
173	  if (!PyLong_Check(ret)) {
174	    PyErr_SetString(PyExc_TypeError, "data_ptr method of Pointer object must return 64-bit int");
175	    ptr_info.valid = false;
176	    goto cleanup;
177	  }
178	  ptr_info.dev_ptr = (hipDeviceptr_t)PyLong_AsUnsignedLongLong(ret);
179	  if(!ptr_info.dev_ptr)
180	    return ptr_info;
181	
182	  // In HIP, device pointers can be used directly
183	  // No need for additional attribute lookup like in CUDA
184	
185	cleanup:
186	  Py_XDECREF(ret);
187	  return ptr_info;
188	}
189	"""
190	
191	LAUNCHER_EPILOGUE = """static PyMethodDef ModuleMethods[] = {
192	  {"launch", PyLaunch, METH_VARARGS, "Entry point for all kernels with this signature"},
193	  {NULL, NULL, 0, NULL} // sentinel
194	};
195	
196	static struct PyModuleDef ModuleDef = {
197	  PyModuleDef_HEAD_INIT,
198	  "__substrate_launcher",
199	  NULL, //documentation
200	  -1, //size
201	  ModuleMethods
202	};
203	
204	PyMODINIT_FUNC PyInit___substrate_launcher(void) {
205	  PyObject *m = PyModule_Create(&ModuleDef);
206	  if(m == NULL) {
207	    return NULL;
208	  }
209	  data_ptr_str = PyUnicode_InternFromString("data_ptr");
210	  if(data_ptr_str == NULL) {{
211	    return NULL;
212	  }}
213	  PyModule_AddFunctions(m, ModuleMethods);
214	  return m;
215	}
216	"""
217	
218	
219	def make_launcher(constants, signature) -> str:
220	    from substrate.language import i32, i64, f32, f64
221	
222	    def ty_to_cpp(ty):
223	        TYPE_MAP = {
224	            "i32": "int",
225	            "i64": "long long",
226	            "fp32": "float",
227	            "float": "float",
228	            "double": "double",
229	            "u32": "unsigned",
230	            "u64": "unsigned long long",
231	        }
232	        if ty[0] == "*":
233	            return "hipDeviceptr_t"
234	        elif ty in TYPE_MAP:
235	            return TYPE_MAP[ty]
236	        else:
237	            raise ValueError(f"Unknown type: {ty}")
238	
239	    # Grid and block dimensions and function
240	    _BASE_ARGS_FORMAT = "iiiiiiKK"
241	
242	    def _flatten_signature(sig, output):
243	        # Flatten tuples
244	        if isinstance(sig, tuple):
245	            for x in sig:
246	                _flatten_signature(x, output)
247	        else:
248	            output.append(sig)
249	
250	    def _extracted_type(ty):
251	        if isinstance(ty, tuple):
252	            val = ",".join(map(_extracted_type, ty))
253	            return f"[{val}]"
254	        if ty[0] == "*":
255	            return "PyObject*"
256	        if ty in "constexpr":
257	            return "PyObject*"
258	        return ty_to_cpp(ty)
259	
260	    def format_of(ty):
261	        FORMAT_MAP = {
262	            "int": "i",
263	            "long long": "L",
264	            "unsigned": "I",
265	            "unsigned long long": "K",
266	            "float": "f",
267	            "double": "d",
268	        }
269	        if ty in "constexpr":
270	            return "O"
271	        if ty[0] == "*":
272	            return "O"
273	        else:
274	            return FORMAT_MAP[ty_to_cpp(ty)]
275	
276	    args_format = "".join([format_of(ty) for ty in signature.values()])
277	    format = _BASE_ARGS_FORMAT + args_format
278	
279	    flat_signature = []
280	    for sig in signature.values():
281	        _flatten_signature(sig, flat_signature)
282	    signature = {i: s for i, s in enumerate(flat_signature)}
283	
284	    args_list = ", " + ", ".join(f"&_arg{i}" for i, _ in signature.items()) if len(signature) > 0 else ""
285	
286	    arg_decl_list = []
287	    for i, ty in signature.items():
288	        if ty == "constexpr":
289	            continue
290	        else:
291	            arg_decl_list.append(f"{ty_to_cpp(ty)} arg{i}")
292	
293	    internal_args_list = []
294	    for i, ty in signature.items():
295	        if ty[0] == "*":
296	            internal_args_list.append(f"ptr_info{i}.dev_ptr")
297	        elif ty != "constexpr":
298	            internal_args_list.append(f"_arg{i}")
299	
300	    ptr_decls = [
301	        f"DevicePtrInfo ptr_info{i} = getPointer(_arg{i}, {i}); if (!ptr_info{i}.valid) return NULL;"
302	        for i, ty in signature.items()
303	        if ty[0] == "*"
304	    ]
305	
306	    # Use struct-based parameter passing like the working HIP test
307	    struct_members = []
308	    for i, ty in signature.items():
309	        if ty != "constexpr":
310	            struct_members.append(f"        {ty_to_cpp(ty)} arg{i};")
311	
312	    struct_decl = "\n".join(struct_members)
313	
314	    struct_assignments = []
315	    for i, ty in signature.items():
316	        if ty != "constexpr":
317	            struct_assignments.append(f"    kernel_params.arg{i} = arg{i};")
318	
319	    struct_assign = "\n".join(struct_assignments)
320	
321	    arg_decl = ", ".join(arg_decl_list)
322	
323	    hip_launch = f"""
324	static void HipLaunch(int grid_x, int grid_y, int grid_z, int block_x, int block_y, int block_z, hipStream_t stream, hipFunction_t func{", " + arg_decl if arg_decl else ""}) {{
325	    // Create parameter structure compatible with HIP
326	    struct {{
327	{struct_decl}
328	    }} kernel_params;
329	
330	    // Fill parameter structure
331	{struct_assign}
332	
333	    size_t paramSize = sizeof(kernel_params);
334	    void *config[] = {{
335	        HIP_LAUNCH_PARAM_BUFFER_POINTER, &kernel_params,
336	        HIP_LAUNCH_PARAM_BUFFER_SIZE, &paramSize,
337	        HIP_LAUNCH_PARAM_END
338	    }};
339	
340	    // Use hipModuleLaunchKernel with config
341	    HIP_CHECK(hipModuleLaunchKernel(func, grid_x, grid_y, grid_z, block_x, block_y, block_z, 0, stream, NULL, config));
342	}}
343	
344	static PyObject *PyLaunch(PyObject *self, PyObject *args) {{
345	    int grid_x, grid_y, grid_z, block_x, block_y, block_z;
346	    unsigned long long stream;
347	    hipFunction_t func;
348	    {"\n".join([f"{_extracted_type(ty)} _arg{i};" for i, ty in signature.items()])}
349	
350	    ensureHipContext();
351	    if (!PyArg_ParseTuple(args, "{format}", &grid_x, &grid_y, &grid_z, &block_x, &block_y, &block_z, &stream, &func {args_list})) {{
352	        return NULL;
353	    }}
354	    {"\n".join(ptr_decls)}
355	
356	    Py_BEGIN_ALLOW_THREADS;
357	    HipLaunch(grid_x, grid_y, grid_z, block_x, block_y, block_z, (hipStream_t)stream, func{", " + ", ".join(internal_args_list) if internal_args_list else ""});
358	    Py_END_ALLOW_THREADS;
359	
360	    if (PyErr_Occurred()) {{
361	      return NULL;
362	    }}
363	    Py_RETURN_NONE;
364	}}
365	"""
366	    # print('DBG', LAUNCHER_PROLOGUE + hip_launch + LAUNCHER_EPILOGUE)
367	    return LAUNCHER_PROLOGUE + hip_launch + LAUNCHER_EPILOGUE
368	
369	
370	class HipLauncher:
371	    def __init__(self, src):
372	        constants = src.constants if hasattr(src, "constants") else dict()
373	        arg_idx = lambda x: (src.fn.arg_names.index(x),) if isinstance(x, str) else x
374	        constants = {arg_idx(idx): value for idx, value in constants.items()}
375	        signature = {idx: value for idx, value in src.signature.items()}
376	        src = make_launcher(constants, signature)
377	        mod = compile_module_from_src(
378	            src,
379	            "__substrate_launcher",
380	            library_dirs=library_dirs(),
381	            include_dirs=include_dirs,
382	            libraries=libraries,
383	            ccflags=["-D__HIP_PLATFORM_AMD__"],
384	        )
385	        self.launch = mod.launch
386	
387	    def __call__(self, gridX, gridY, gridZ, blockX, blockY, blockZ, stream, function, *args):
388	        return self.launch(gridX, gridY, gridZ, blockX, blockY, blockZ, stream, function, *args)
389	
390	
391	class AmdgpuDriver(GPUDriver):
392	    def __init__(self):
393	        super().__init__()
394	        self.launcher_cls = HipLauncher
395	        self.utils = HipUtils()
396	
397	    @classmethod
398	    def is_active(cls):
399	        # Check for AMD GPU availability via HIP
400	        try:
401	            # First try to detect ROCm/HIP installation
402	            subprocess.check_output(["hipcc", "--version"], stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL)
403	            return True
404	        except (subprocess.CalledProcessError, FileNotFoundError):
405	            # Fallback: try torch.cuda with ROCm backend
406	            try:
407	                import torch
408	
409	                if not torch.cuda.is_available():
410	                    return False
411	                # For ROCm builds, check if hipcc is available
412	                subprocess.run(["hipcc", "--version"], stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL, stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL, check=True)
413	                return True
414	            except (subprocess.CalledProcessError, FileNotFoundError, ImportError):
415	                return False
416	
417	    @classmethod
418	    def get_current_target(cls):
419	        if not cls.is_active():
420	            raise RuntimeError("No HIP device available for AmdgpuDriver.")
421	
422	        arch = "gfx90a"
423	
424	        try:
425	            result = subprocess.check_output(["rocm-smi", "--showproductname"], stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL).decode()
426	            lowered = result.lower()
427	            if "gfx906" in lowered:
428	                arch = "gfx906"
429	            elif "gfx908" in lowered:
430	                arch = "gfx908"
431	            elif "gfx90a" in lowered:
432	                arch = "gfx90a"
433	            if "gfx942" in lowered:
434	                arch = "gfx942"
435	            elif "gfx1100" in lowered:
436	                arch = "gfx1100"
437	        except subprocess.CalledProcessError:
438	            pass  # Use default arch if detection fails
439	
440	        return GPUTarget("amdgcn-amd-amdhsa", arch)
441

from __future__ import annotations

import functools
import importlib.util
import os
import shutil
import subprocess
import sysconfig
import tempfile

from types import ModuleType


def _build(name: str, src: str, srcdir: str, library_dirs: list[str], include_dirs: list[str], libraries: list[str],
           ccflags: list[str]) -> str:
    suffix = sysconfig.get_config_var('EXT_SUFFIX')
    so = os.path.join(srcdir, '{name}{suffix}'.format(name=name, suffix=suffix))
    # try to avoid setuptools if possible
    cc = os.environ.get("CC")
    if cc is None:
        clang = shutil.which("clang")
        gcc = shutil.which("gcc")
        cc = gcc if gcc is not None else clang
        if cc is None:
            raise RuntimeError(
                "Failed to find C compiler. Please specify via CC environment variable or set triton.knobs.build.impl.")
    # This function was renamed and made public in Python 3.10
    if hasattr(sysconfig, 'get_default_scheme'):
        scheme = sysconfig.get_default_scheme()
    else:
        scheme = sysconfig._get_default_scheme()  # type: ignore
    # 'posix_local' is a custom scheme on Debian. However, starting Python 3.10, the default install
    # path changes to include 'local'. This change is required to use triton with system-wide python.
    if scheme == 'posix_local':
        scheme = 'posix_prefix'
    py_include_dir = sysconfig.get_paths(scheme=scheme)["include"]
    include_dirs = include_dirs + [srcdir, py_include_dir]
    # for -Wno-psabi, see https://gcc.gnu.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=111047
    cc_cmd = [cc, src, "-O3", "-shared", "-fPIC", "-Wno-psabi", "-o", so]
    cc_cmd += [f'-l{lib}' for lib in libraries]
    cc_cmd += [f"-L{dir}" for dir in library_dirs]
    cc_cmd += [f"-I{dir}" for dir in include_dirs if dir is not None]
    cc_cmd.extend(ccflags)
    subprocess.check_call(cc_cmd, stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL)
    return so


@functools.lru_cache
def platform_key() -> str:
    from platform import machine, system, architecture
    return ",".join([machine(), system(), *architecture()])


def _load_module_from_path(name: str, path: str) -> ModuleType:
    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(name, path)
    if not spec or not spec.loader:
        raise RuntimeError(f"Failed to load newly compiled {name} from {path}")
    mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
    spec.loader.exec_module(mod)
    return mod


def compile_module_from_src(src: str, name: str, library_dirs: list[str] | None = None,
                            include_dirs: list[str] | None = None, libraries: list[str] | None = None,
                            ccflags: list[str] | None = None) -> ModuleType:
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        src_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, name + ".c")
        with open(src_path, "w") as f:
            f.write(src)
        so = _build(name, src_path, tmpdir, library_dirs or [], include_dirs or [], libraries or [], ccflags or [])
        return _load_module_from_path(name, so)

12	class AmdgpuCompiler(BaseBackend):
13	    @staticmethod
14	    def supports_target(target: GPUTarget):
15	        return target.tuple == "amdgcn-amd-amdhsa"
16	
17	    def parse_options(self, options) -> object:
18	        if options is None:
19	            return AmdgpuCompilerOptions()
20	        if isinstance(options, AmdgpuCompilerOptions):
21	            return options
22	
23	        args = {}
24	        if "num_warps" in options and options["num_warps"] is not None:
25	            args["num_warps"] = options["num_warps"]
26	        return AmdgpuCompilerOptions(**args)

29	        if options is None:
30	            options = self.parse_options({})
31	        elif isinstance(options, dict):
32	            options = self.parse_options(options)
33	        return compile_to_binary(src, target, opt_level=2, options=options)

1	import ast
2	import json

8	class _LocalNameCollector(ast.NodeVisitor):
9	    def __init__(self) -> None:
10	        self.names = set()
11	
12	    def _add_target(self, target):
13	        if isinstance(target, ast.Name):
14	            self.names.add(target.id)
15	            return
16	        if isinstance(target, (ast.Tuple, ast.List)):
17	            for elt in target.elts:
18	                self._add_target(elt)
19	            return
20	        if isinstance(target, ast.Starred):
21	            self._add_target(target.value)
22	
23	    def visit_FunctionDef(self, node):
24	        self.names.add(node.name)
25	
26	    def visit_AsyncFunctionDef(self, node):
27	        self.names.add(node.name)
28	
29	    def visit_ClassDef(self, node):
30	        self.names.add(node.name)
31	
32	    def visit_Assign(self, node):
33	        for target in node.targets:
34	            self._add_target(target)
35	        self.generic_visit(node.value)
36	
37	    def visit_AnnAssign(self, node):
38	        self._add_target(node.target)
39	        if node.value is not None:
40	            self.generic_visit(node.value)
41	
42	    def visit_AugAssign(self, node):
43	        self._add_target(node.target)
44	        self.generic_visit(node.value)
45	
46	    def visit_For(self, node):
47	        self._add_target(node.target)
48	        self.generic_visit(node)
49	
50	    def visit_AsyncFor(self, node):
51	        self._add_target(node.target)
52	        self.generic_visit(node)
53	
54	    def visit_With(self, node):
55	        for item in node.items:
56	            if item.optional_vars is not None:
57	                self._add_target(item.optional_vars)
58	        self.generic_visit(node)
59	
60	    def visit_AsyncWith(self, node):
61	        for item in node.items:
62	            if item.optional_vars is not None:
63	                self._add_target(item.optional_vars)
64	        self.generic_visit(node)
65	
66	    def visit_ExceptHandler(self, node):
67	        if node.name:
68	            self.names.add(node.name)
69	        self.generic_visit(node)
70	
71	    def visit_Import(self, node):
72	        for alias in node.names:
73	            self.names.add(alias.asname or alias.name.split(".")[0])
74	
75	    def visit_ImportFrom(self, node):
76	        for alias in node.names:
77	            self.names.add(alias.asname or alias.name)

79	    def visit_NamedExpr(self, node):

84	def _serialize_constexprs(src) -> str:
85	    constants = getattr(src, "constants", None) or {}
86	    global_constants = getattr(src, "global_constants", None) or {}
87	    if not constants and not global_constants:
88	        return "[]"
89	    constexprs_list = []
90	    for k, info in constants.items():
91	        param_name = src.fn.arg_names[k[0]]
92	        constexprs_list.append(
93	            {
94	                "name": param_name,
95	                "type": info["type"],
96	                "value": info["value"],
97	            }
98	        )
99	    for name, info in global_constants.items():
100	        constexprs_list.append(
101	            {
102	                "name": name,
103	                "type": info["type"],
104	                "value": info["value"],
105	            }
106	        )
107	    return json.dumps(constexprs_list)

110	def _serialize_global_constexprs(global_constants) -> str:
111	    if not global_constants:
112	        return "[]"
113	    constexprs_list = []
114	    for name, info in global_constants.items():
115	        constexprs_list.append(
116	            {
117	                "name": name,
118	                "type": info["type"],
119	                "value": info["value"],
120	            }
121	        )

125	def _get_function_def(py_module: ast.AST) -> ast.FunctionDef:
126	    for node in getattr(py_module, "body", []):
127	        if isinstance(node, ast.FunctionDef):
128	            return node
129	    raise ValueError("FunctionDef not found in parsed AST")

132	class DependenciesFinder(ast.NodeVisitor):
133	    def __init__(self, jit_callable):
134	        self.jit_callable = jit_callable
135	        self.capture = jit_callable.get_capture_scope()
136	        self.dependencies = []
137	        self._locals_stack = []
138	
139	    def _record_dependency(self, target):
140	        from substrate.runtime.jit import JITCallable
141	
142	        if isinstance(target, JITCallable) and target is not self.jit_callable:
143	            self.dependencies.append(target)
144	
145	    def _collect_local_names(self, node):
146	        names = set()
147	        if isinstance(node, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef, ast.Lambda)):
148	            args = node.args
149	            for arg in args.posonlyargs + args.args + args.kwonlyargs:
150	                names.add(arg.arg)
151	            if args.vararg is not None:
152	                names.add(args.vararg.arg)
153	            if args.kwarg is not None:
154	                names.add(args.kwarg.arg)
155	
156	        collector = _LocalNameCollector()
157	        if isinstance(node, ast.Lambda):
158	            collector.visit(node.body)
159	        else:
160	            for stmt in node.body:
161	                collector.visit(stmt)
162	        names.update(collector.names)
163	        return names
164	
165	    def _push_local_names(self, node):
166	        self._locals_stack.append(self._collect_local_names(node))
167	
168	    def _pop_local_names(self):
169	        self._locals_stack.pop()
170	
171	    def _is_local_name(self, name):
172	        return any(name in scope for scope in self._locals_stack)
173	
174	    def visit_Call(self, node: ast.Call):
175	        if isinstance(node.func, ast.Name):
176	            if self._is_local_name(node.func.id):
177	                self.generic_visit(node)
178	                return
179	            target = self.capture.get(node.func.id)
180	            if target is not None:
181	                self._record_dependency(target)
182	        elif isinstance(node.func, ast.Attribute):
183	            if isinstance(node.func.value, ast.Name):
184	                if self._is_local_name(node.func.value.id):
185	                    self.generic_visit(node)
186	                    return
187	                base = self.capture.get(node.func.value.id)
188	                if base is not None and hasattr(base, node.func.attr):
189	                    self._record_dependency(getattr(base, node.func.attr))
190	        self.generic_visit(node)
191	
192	    def visit_FunctionDef(self, node):
193	        self._push_local_names(node)
194	        self.generic_visit(node)
195	        self._pop_local_names()
196	
197	    def visit_AsyncFunctionDef(self, node):
198	        self._push_local_names(node)
199	        self.generic_visit(node)
200	        self._pop_local_names()
201	
202	    def visit_Lambda(self, node):
203	        self._push_local_names(node)
204	        self.generic_visit(node)
205	        self._pop_local_names()
206	
207	
208	def _collect_jit_dependencies(jit_fn) -> list:
209	    from substrate.runtime.jit import JITCallable
210	
211	    seen = set()
212	    ordered = []
213	
214	    def visit(current):
215	        finder = DependenciesFinder(current)
216	        finder.visit(current.parse())
217	        for dep in finder.dependencies:
218	            if dep in seen:
219	                continue
220	            seen.add(dep)
221	            visit(dep)
222	            ordered.append(dep)
223	
224	    if not isinstance(jit_fn, JITCallable):
225	        return []
226	
227	    visit(jit_fn)
228	    return ordered
229	
230	
231	def _build_import_module(jit_fns: list) -> ast.Module:
232	    import_nodes = []
233	    seen_imports = set()
234	
235	    for fn in jit_fns:
236	        py_module = fn.parse()
237	        used_names = {node.id for node in ast.walk(py_module) if isinstance(node, ast.Name)}
238	        capture = fn.get_capture_scope()
239	        for name in sorted(used_names):
240	            value = capture.get(name)
241	            if isinstance(value, types.ModuleType):
242	                module_name = value.__name__
243	                key = (module_name, name if name != module_name else None)
244	                if key in seen_imports:
245	                    continue
246	                seen_imports.add(key)
247	                asname = None if name == module_name else name
248	                import_nodes.append(
249	                    ast.Import(names=[ast.alias(name=module_name, asname=asname)])
250	                )
251	
252	    module = ast.Module(body=import_nodes, type_ignores=[])
253	    return ast.fix_missing_locations(module)
254	
255	
256	def compile_to_binary(src, target, opt_level: int = 2, options=None):
257	    constexprs_json = _serialize_constexprs(src)
258	
259	    jit_deps = _collect_jit_dependencies(src.fn)
260	    import_module = _build_import_module([src.fn, *jit_deps])
261	
262	    generator = _C.MLIRGenerator()
263	    generator.generate_from_python_ast(import_module)
264	
265	    for dep in jit_deps:
266	        generator.add_jit_dependency(dep.parse())
267	
268	    for dep in jit_deps:
269	        dep_func = _get_function_def(dep.parse())
270	        dep_globals = {}
271	        collect_globals = getattr(dep, "_collect_global_constexprs", None)
272	        if callable(collect_globals):
273	            dep_globals = collect_globals()
274	        generator.visit_function_def(
275	            dep_func, _serialize_global_constexprs(dep_globals), "jit"
276	        )
277	
278	    kernel_func = _get_function_def(src.fn.parse())
279	    generator.visit_function_def(kernel_func, constexprs_json, "kernel")
280	
281	    num_warps = getattr(options, "num_warps", -1)
282	    return generator.compile_to_binary_bytes(
283	        target.tuple, target.chip, opt_level, num_warps
284	    )
285

I see that the compilation is done via a C++ binding `_C.MLIRGenerator`. The kernel is compiled to binary bytes directly. To check if MFMA instructions are being used, I need to either:
1. Use a disassembler on the compiled binary
2. Check if there's an option in the substrate bindings to dump assembly

Let me try to use rocm's disassembler on the compiled kernel. First, let me check how the compiled binary is stored/used.

105	class DependenciesFinder(ast.NodeVisitor):
106	    """
107	    This AST visitor is used to find dependencies of a JITFunction. This can
108	    be used to invalidate a JITFunction's hash when its source code -- or
109	    that of its dependencies -- changes.
110	
111	    This visitor also keeps track of the global variables touched by the
112	    JITFunction.  When we launch the kernel, we check that these have the same
113	    values as they did when we ran this visitor.  If not, we raise an error (or
114	    otherwise we could recompile).
115	    """
116	
117	    def __init__(self, name, globals, nonlocals, src) -> None:
118	        super().__init__()
119	        self.name = name
120	        self.hasher = hashlib.sha256(src.encode("utf-8"))
121	
122	        # This function's __globals__ dict.
123	        self.globals = globals
124	        self.nonlocals = nonlocals
125	
126	        # Python builtins that can be accessed from Triton kernels.
127	        self.supported_python_builtins = {
128	            'float',
129	            'getattr',
130	            'int',
131	            'isinstance',
132	            'len',
133	            'list',
134	            'max',
135	            'min',
136	            'print',
137	            'range',
138	        }
139	        self.supported_modules = {
140	            SUBSTRATE_MODULE,
141	            "copy",
142	            "math",
143	        }
144	
145	        # used_global_vals tells us which global variables are used by this
146	        # function and all those it transitively calls, plus the values of those
147	        # variables when each function was initially run.  (That is, if A calls
148	        # C, and B calls C, then the values for C in used_global_vals will be
149	        # from the first time C was run, either by A or B.)
150	        #
151	        # Each function may have a different __globals__ dict, so the global
152	        # variable `foo` may actually have a different value in the different
153	        # functions.  Thus this map is actually
154	        #  (var_name, id(__globals__)) -> (var_value, __globals__).
155	        self.used_global_vals: Dict[Tuple[str, int], Tuple[Any, Dict[str, Any]]] = {}
156	
157	        self.visiting_arg_default_value = False
158	        self._locals_stack = []
159	
160	    @property
161	    def ret(self):
162	        return self.hasher.hexdigest()
163	
164	    @staticmethod
165	    def _values_equal(v1, v2):
166	        try:
167	            eq = v1 == v2
168	        except Exception:
169	            return False
170	        if eq is NotImplemented:
171	            return False
172	        if isinstance(eq, bool):
173	            return eq
174	        try:
175	            return bool(eq)
176	        except Exception:
177	            pass
178	        try:
179	            return bool(eq.all())
180	        except Exception:
181	            return False
182	    def _update_hash(self, func):
183	        assert isinstance(func, JITCallable)
184	        # Merge our used_global_vals with those of the called function,
185	        # after checking that all overlapping values are consistent.
186	        for k in self.used_global_vals.keys() & func.used_global_vals.keys():
187	            var_name, _ = k
188	            v1, _ = self.used_global_vals[k]
189	            v2, _ = func.used_global_vals[k]
190	            if not self._values_equal(v1, v2):
191	                raise RuntimeError(
192	                    f"Global variable {var_name} has value {v1} when compiling {self.name}, but inner kernel {func.__name__} has conflicting value {v2} from when it was first compiled.  This is not allowed."
193	                )
194	        self.used_global_vals.update(func.used_global_vals)
195	        # update hash
196	        func_key = func.cache_key
197	        func_key += str(getattr(func, "noinline", False))
198	        self.hasher.update(func_key.encode("utf-8"))
199	
200	    def _collect_local_names(self, node):
201	        names = set()
202	        if isinstance(node, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef, ast.Lambda)):
203	            args = node.args
204	            for arg in args.posonlyargs + args.args + args.kwonlyargs:
205	                names.add(arg.arg)
206	            if args.vararg is not None:
207	                names.add(args.vararg.arg)
208	            if args.kwarg is not None:
209	                names.add(args.kwarg.arg)
210	
211	        collector = _LocalNameCollector()
212	        if isinstance(node, ast.Lambda):
213	            collector.visit(node.body)
214	        else:
215	            for stmt in node.body:
216	                collector.visit(stmt)
217	        names.update(collector.names)
218	        return names
219	
220	    def _push_local_names(self, node):
221	        self._locals_stack.append(self._collect_local_names(node))
222	
223	    def _pop_local_names(self):
224	        self._locals_stack.pop()
225	
226	    def _is_local_name(self, name):
227	        return any(name in scope for scope in self._locals_stack)
228	
229	    def record_reference(self, val, var_dict=None, name=None):
230	        from ..language.core import constexpr
231	        # Only keep track of "interesting" global variables, that non-evil users
232	        # might change.  Don't consider functions, modules, builtins, etc.  This
233	        # helps keep the list of vars we have to check small.
234	        if val is None or type(val) is ModuleType:
235	            return
236	
237	        # if getattr(val, "__triton_aggregate__", False):
238	        #     for attr in val.hash_attrs:
239	        #         self.record_reference(attr)
240	        #     return
241	
242	        # if getattr(val, "__triton_builtin__", False):
243	        #     return
244	
245	        # # Stubs that aren't real functions
246	        # if getattr(val, "__module__", "") == "triton.language.extra.libdevice":
247	        #     return
248	
249	        if isinstance(val, JITCallable):
250	            self._update_hash(val)
251	            return
252	
253	        if callable(val) and not isinstance(val, type) and not isinstance(val, constexpr):
254	            raise RuntimeError(
255	                f"Unsupported function referenced: {val}. Functions called from @substrate.jit "
256	                "must be decorated with @substrate.jit."
257	            )
258	
259	        # Python default arguments are resolved only once, when the
260	        # function is defined.  So if you do `foo(a=A)` and the value of
261	        # A changes, foo will still use the old value of A.
262	        # It would be pretty evil if someone did `import x` and then
263	        # `x = blah`.
264	        if self.visiting_arg_default_value:
265	            return
266	
267	        if var_dict is not None:
268	            try:
269	                stored_val = copy.deepcopy(val)
270	            except Exception:
271	                stored_val = val
272	            self.used_global_vals[(name, id(var_dict))] = (stored_val, var_dict)
273	        return
274	
275	    def visit_Name(self, node):
276	        if type(node.ctx) is ast.Store:
277	            return node.id
278	
279	        if self._is_local_name(node.id):
280	            # The global name is hidden by the local name.
281	            return None
282	
283	        def name_lookup(name):
284	            val = self.globals.get(name, None)
285	            if val is not None:
286	                return val, self.globals
287	            val = self.nonlocals.get(name, None)
288	            if val is not None:
289	                return val, self.nonlocals
290	            return None, None
291	
292	        val, var_dict = name_lookup(node.id)
293	        if node.id in self.supported_python_builtins:
294	            return val
295	
296	        self.record_reference(val, var_dict, node.id)
297	        return val
298	
299	    def visit_Tuple(self, node):
300	        # We need to explicitly return the tuple values so that visit_Assign can
301	        # access them in the case of `a, b = ...`.
302	        return [self.visit(elt) for elt in node.elts]
303	
304	    def visit_Attribute(self, node):
305	        lhs = self.visit(node.value)
306	        while isinstance(lhs, ast.Attribute):
307	            lhs = self.visit(lhs.value)
308	        lhs_name = getattr(lhs, "__name__", "")
309	        if lhs is None or lhs_name in self.supported_modules:
310	            return None
311	        ret = getattr(lhs, node.attr)
312	        self.record_reference(ret)
313	        return ret
314	
315	    def visit_FunctionDef(self, node):
316	        self._push_local_names(node)
317	        self.generic_visit(node)
318	        self._pop_local_names()
319	
320	    def visit_AsyncFunctionDef(self, node):
321	        self._push_local_names(node)
322	        self.generic_visit(node)
323	        self._pop_local_names()
324	
325	    def visit_Lambda(self, node):
326	        self._push_local_names(node)
327	        self.generic_visit(node)
328	        self._pop_local_names()
329	
330	    def visit_arguments(self, node):
331	        # The purpose of this function is to visit everything in `arguments`
332	        # just like `generic_visit`, except when we're visiting default values
333	        # (i.e. the `foo` part of `def fn(x = foo)`), we set
334	        # self.visiting_arg_default_value = True.  This allows visit_Name to be
335	        # aware that we're inside function default values, which have special
336	        # semantics.
337	
338	        # According to the AST docs, the arguments node has the following structure.
339	        #
340	        # arguments = (arg* posonlyargs, arg* args, arg? vararg, arg* kwonlyargs,
341	        #              expr* kw_defaults, arg? kwarg, expr* defaults)
342	        def visit_defaults(defaults):
343	            try:

399	def create_function_from_signature(sig, kparams, backend):
400	    """
401	    Equivalent to sig.bind followed by apply_defaults. This generates a
402	    native Python function (using exec) which can be memoized on a per-kernel
403	    basis to avoid having to run these expensive functions -- which constitute
404	    much of the kernel launch overhead -- every time we run the kernel.
405	    """
406	    assert len(sig.parameters) == len(kparams)
407	    # Create the function argument list and the dict entries for the return statement
408	    specialization = []
409	    # signature
410	    for name, kp in zip(sig.parameters.keys(), kparams):
411	        if kp.is_constexpr:
412	            specialization.append(f'("constexpr", {name})')
413	        else:
414	            is_const = "True" if kp.is_const else "False"
415	            specialize = "False" if kp.do_not_specialize else "True"
416	            align = "False" if kp.do_not_specialize_on_alignment else "True"
417	            ret = f"specialize_impl(backend, {name}, {is_const}, {specialize}, {align})"
418	            if kp.annotation_type:
419	                if isinstance(kp.annotation_type, str):
420	                    if kp.annotation_type == "u1" or kp.annotation_type[:2] in ["fp", "bf"]:
421	                        # we do not specialize non-constexpr floats and bools:
422	                        specialize = False
423	                if specialize:
424	                    specialization.append(f'("{kp.annotation_type}",) + {ret}[1:]')
425	                else:
426	                    # skip runtime specialization:
427	                    specialization.append(f'("{kp.annotation_type}", None)')
428	            else:
429	                specialization.append(f"{ret}")
430	
431	    # compute argument string for a given parameter
432	    arg = lambda x: x[0] if x[1].default is inspect.Parameter.empty else f"{x[0]}=default_{x[0]}"
433	    func_body = f"""
434	def dynamic_func({", ".join(list(map(arg, sig.parameters.items())) + ["**options"])}):
435	    params = {{{", ".join([f"'{name}': {name}" for name in sig.parameters.keys()])}}}
436	    specialization = [{",".join(specialization)}]
437	    return params, specialization, options
438	"""
439	
440	    # Prepare defaults to be inserted into function namespace
441	    func_namespace = {
442	        f"default_{name}": param.default
443	        for name, param in sig.parameters.items()
444	        if param.default is not inspect.Parameter.empty
445	    }
446	
447	    # TODO: Use native_specialize_impl to improve performance
448	    from .specialize import reference_specialize_impl
449	
450	    specialize_impl = reference_specialize_impl
451	    func_namespace["specialize_impl"] = specialize_impl
452	    func_namespace["backend"] = backend
453	    func_namespace["JITCallable"] = JITCallable
454	
455	    # Execute the function string in func_namespace to create the function
456	    exec(func_body, func_namespace)
457	
458	    # Extract the newly created function from the namespace
459	    return func_namespace["dynamic_func"]

463	    return f"{fn.__module__}.{fn.__qualname__}"

466	def get_jit_fn_file_line(fn):
467	    base_fn = fn
468	    while not isinstance(base_fn, JITCallable):
469	        base_fn = base_fn.fn
470	    file_name = base_fn.fn.__code__.co_filename
471	    begin_line = base_fn.starting_line_number
472	    # Match the following pattern:
473	    # @triton.autotune(...) <- foo.__code__.co_firstlineno
474	    # @triton.heuristics(...)
475	    # @triton.jit
476	    # def foo(...): <- this line is the first line
477	    for idx, line in enumerate(base_fn.raw_src):
478	        if line.strip().startswith("def "):
479	            begin_line += idx
480	            break
481	    return file_name, begin_line

484	def _attach_ast_source_metadata(tree, file_name: str, source_code: str):
485	    tree._substrate_file_name = file_name
486	    tree._substrate_source_code = source_code
487	    if len(tree.body) == 1 and isinstance(tree.body[0], ast.FunctionDef):
488	        tree.body[0]._substrate_file_name = file_name
489	        tree.body[0]._substrate_source_code = source_code

493	class KernelInterface(Generic[T]):
494	    run: T
495	
496	    def __getitem__(self, dims) -> T:
497	        """
498	        A JIT function is launched with: fn[grid](*args, **kwargs).
499	        Hence JITFunction.__getitem__ returns a callable proxy that
500	        memorizes the grid.
501	        """
502	        return lambda *args, **kwargs: self.run(dims, *args, **kwargs)

505	class JITCallable:
506	    def __init__(self, fn: Callable):
507	        self.fn = fn
508	        self.signature = inspect.signature(fn)
509	        try:
510	            self.raw_src, self.starting_line_number = inspect.getsourcelines(fn)
511	        except OSError as e:
512	            raise ValueError("@jit functions should be defined in a Python file") from e
513	        self._fn_name = get_full_name(fn)
514	        self._hash_lock = threading.RLock()
515	
516	        src = textwrap.dedent("".join(self.raw_src))
517	        src = src[re.search(r"^def\s+\w+\s*\(", src, re.MULTILINE).start() :]
518	        self._src = src
519	        self.hash = None
520	
521	        # Reuse docs of wrapped function
522	        self.__doc__ = fn.__doc__
523	        self.__name__ = fn.__name__
524	        self.__qualname__ = fn.__qualname__
525	        self.__globals__ = fn.__globals__
526	        self.__module__ = fn.__module__
527	
528	        # Map of global variables used by the function and any functions it
529	        # transitively calls, plus their values.  The values are collected when
530	        # the function is first compiled.  Then every time we run the function,
531	        # we check that the values of the globals match what's expected,
532	        # otherwise we raise an error.
533	        #
534	        # Different functions can have different __globals__ maps, so the map
535	        # key is actually (var name, id(__globals__)), and the map value is
536	        # (value, __globals__).
537	        self.used_global_vals: Dict[Tuple[str, int], Tuple[Any, Dict[str, Any]]] = {}
538	
539	    def get_capture_scope(self):
540	        fn = self.fn
541	        if fn.__closure__ is None:
542	            return self.__globals__
543	        nonlocals = {name: cell.cell_contents for name, cell in zip(fn.__code__.co_freevars, fn.__closure__)}
544	        return self.__globals__ | nonlocals
545	
546	    @property
547	    def cache_key(self) -> str:
548	        # TODO : hash should be attribute of `self`
549	        with self._hash_lock:
550	            if self.hash is not None:
551	                return self.hash
552	            # Set a placeholder hash to break recursion in case the function
553	            # transitively calls itself. The full hash is set after.
554	            self.hash = f"recursion:{self._fn_name}"
555	            nonlocals = inspect.getclosurevars(self.fn).nonlocals
556	            dependencies_finder = DependenciesFinder(name=self._fn_name, globals=self.__globals__, nonlocals=nonlocals,
557	                                                     src=self.src)
558	            dependencies_finder.visit(self.parse())
559	            self.hash = dependencies_finder.ret + str(self.starting_line_number)
560	            self.used_global_vals = dict(sorted(dependencies_finder.used_global_vals.items()))
561	
562	            from ..language.core import constexpr
563	            self.hash += str([(name, val)
564	                              for (name, _), (val, _) in self.used_global_vals.items()
565	                              if isinstance(val, constexpr)])
566	            self.hash = hashlib.sha256(self.hash.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
567	        return self.hash
568	
569	    def __hash__(self):
570	        return hash(self.cache_key)
571	
572	    def parse(self):
573	        file_name, begin_line = get_jit_fn_file_line(self)
574	        padded_source = ("\n" * max(begin_line - 1, 0)) + self._src
575	        tree = ast.parse(self._src, filename=file_name)
576	        if begin_line > 1:
577	            tree = ast.increment_lineno(tree, begin_line - 1)
578	        tree = _attach_ast_source_metadata(tree, file_name, padded_source)
579	        assert isinstance(tree, ast.Module)
580	        assert len(tree.body) == 1
581	        assert isinstance(tree.body[0], ast.FunctionDef)
582	        return tree
583	
584	    def _set_src(self, new_src: str):
585	        raise AttributeError("Cannot set attribute 'src' directly. "
586	                             "Use '_unsafe_update_src()' and manually clear `.hash` of all callers"
587	                             "instead.")
588	
589	    def _get_src(self):
590	        return self._src
591	
592	    def _unsafe_update_src(self, new_src: str):
593	        self._src = new_src
594	        self.hash = None
595	        self.used_global_vals = {}
596	
597	    src = property(fget=_get_src, fset=_set_src)

600	def _normalize_ty(ty) -> str:
601	    from ..language import core
602	
603	    if isinstance(ty, str):
604	        ty = ty.strip()
605	        if ty.startswith("const "):
606	            ty = ty.removeprefix("const")
607	            ty = _normalize_ty(ty)
608	            assert ty.startswith("*")
609	            return "*k" + ty[1:]
610	        if ty.endswith("*"):
611	            return "*" + _normalize_ty(ty[:-1])
612	        if ty.startswith("*"):
613	            return "*" + _normalize_ty(ty[1:])
614	        if ty.startswith("S."):
615	            return _normalize_ty(ty.removeprefix("S."))
616	    elif isinstance(ty, core.pointer_type):
617	        return f"*{_normalize_ty(ty.element_ty)}"
618	    elif isinstance(ty, core.dtype):
619	        ty = ty.name
620	    elif isinstance(ty, type):
621	        ty = ty.__name__
622	    else:
623	        ty = str(ty)
624	    return type_canonicalisation_dict.get(ty.replace("_t", ""), ty)

666	def compute_cache_key(kernel_key_cache, specialization, options):
667	    key = (tuple(specialization), str(options))
668	    cache_key = kernel_key_cache.get(key, None)
669	    if cache_key is not None:
670	        return cache_key

672	    # Replace JITCallable objects with their hash, so the cache key will change if the src is updated
673	    def replace_callables(obj):
674	        if isinstance(obj, list):
675	            return [replace_callables(arg) for arg in obj]
676	        elif is_namedtuple(obj):
677	            results = [replace_callables(arg) for arg in obj]
678	            return obj.__class__(*results)
679	        elif isinstance(obj, tuple):
680	            return tuple(replace_callables(arg) for arg in obj)
681	        elif isinstance(obj, JITCallable):
682	            return obj.cache_key
683	        return obj
684	
685	    cache_key = str(replace_callables(specialization)) + str(options)
686	    kernel_key_cache[key] = cache_key

691	class JITFunction(JITCallable, KernelInterface[T]):

705	    def _infer_constexpr_type(self, value):
706	        """Infer MLIR type string from Python value."""
707	        if isinstance(value, bool):
708	            return "i1"
709	        elif isinstance(value, int):
710	            if -2147483648 <= value <= 2147483647:
711	                return "i32"
712	            else:
713	                return "i64"
714	        elif isinstance(value, float):
715	            return "f64"
716	        else:
717	            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported constexpr type: {type(value)}")
718	
719	    def _collect_global_constexprs(self, skip_names=None):
720	        _ = self.cache_key
721	        constexprs = {}
722	        skip = set(skip_names or [])
723	        for (name, _), (value, _) in self.used_global_vals.items():
724	            if name in skip:
725	                continue
726	            if hasattr(value, "value"):
727	                value = value.value
728	            try:
729	                constexpr_type = self._infer_constexpr_type(value)
730	            except ValueError:
731	                continue
732	            constexprs[name] = {"type": constexpr_type, "value": value}

735	    def create_binder(self):

743	        self.compile = compile
744	        self.ASTSource = ASTSource
745	        binder = create_function_from_signature(self.signature, self.params, backend)

748	    def _pack_args(self, backend, kwargs, bound_args, specialization, options):
749	        # options
750	        options = backend.parse_options(options)
751	        # signature
752	        sigkeys = [x.name for x in self.params]
753	        sigvals = [x[0] for x in specialization]
754	        signature = {k: v for (k, v) in zip(sigkeys, sigvals)}
755	        # check arguments
756	        assert "device_type" not in kwargs, "device_type option is deprecated; current target will be used"

763	        constexpr_paths = find_paths_if(sigvals, lambda _, val: val == "constexpr")
764	        constexprs = {}
765	        for path in constexpr_paths:
766	            param_idx = path[0]

768	            value = get_iterable_path(list(bound_args.values()), path)

770	            if hasattr(value, "value"):
771	                value = value.value
772	            constexpr_type = self._infer_constexpr_type(value)
773	            constexprs[param_name] = {"type": constexpr_type, "value": value}
774	        global_constexprs = self._collect_global_constexprs(constexprs.keys())

780	    def run(self, dims, *args, **kwargs):
781	        # parse options
782	        device = driver.active.get_current_device()
783	        stream = driver.active.get_current_stream(device)

790	        grid, block = dims()

792	        options, signature, constexprs, global_constexprs, attrs = self._pack_args(
793	            backend, kwargs, bound_args, specialization, options
794	        )

796	        key = compute_cache_key(kernel_key_cache, specialization, options)
797	        kernel = kernel_cache.get(key, None)

818	            block[2],

843	    else:

I can see that the kernel is using `v_mul_f32` and `v_add_f32` instructions, which are scalar floating-point multiply and add operations. There are no `v_mfma` instructions in the generated code. This confirms that the current implementation is not using MFMA instructions.

The kernel uses:
- `v_mul_f32` - scalar floating-point multiply
- `v_add_f32` - scalar floating-point add

Instead of:
- `v_mfma_f32_16x16x16f16` - MFMA instruction that does matrix multiply-add

This is expected given the earlier discussion - the kernel uses regular f32 multiply-add operations because we couldn't get the MFMA bit packing to work with substrate's type system.

Now I need to try to implement actual MFMA instructions. The challenge was packing f16 values into u32 for the MFMA instruction. Let me look at the test_gemm_mfma.py more carefully to see how they handle this, and try to implement actual MFMA in the depthwise conv kernel.

Looking at the test, they pack f16 values into u32 outside the kernel (in host code), and then use S.view to reinterpret the bits inside the kernel. The key is that the input to the kernel is already packed as u32.

For the depthwise conv, I need to:
1. Pack f16 inputs into u32 before calling the kernel
2. Use S.view inside the kernel to get the f16 values
3. Call S.amdgpu.mfma_16x16x16_f16_f32

But the challenge is that the depthwise conv reads scattered memory locations (not contiguous like a GEMM), so packing is tricky.

Actually, looking at the MFMA instruction requirements more carefully - MFMA expects a matrix multiply pattern where each thread holds specific elements. For depthwise conv, the memory access pattern is scattered and doesn't naturally fit the MFMA input layout. 

This is a fundamental mismatch. MFMA shines in dense matrix multiplications, but depthwise convolution with a 3x3 kernel involves scattered memory reads - each output element multiplies one input element by one weight, accumulating across 9 kernel positions. This doesn't form the matrix blocks that MFMA requires.

The current tiled implementation is reasonable for this problem - it processes 16x16 output tiles per warp with 64 threads, which aligns with the MFMA thread count, even though it can't use MFMA instructions.

I could try reshaping the problem into a GEMM to leverage MFMA, but the overhead would likely outweigh any benefit. The better approach might be exploring other AMD GPU instructions or accepting this limitation. Let me reconsider what the constraints actually allowed - the requirement was to use MFMA if possible, not mandatory. I should investigate whether there's a way to reorganize the depthwise conv computation to enable MFMA, perhaps by processing multiple output channels or input patches in a block structure.

32	N_TILES_PER_WARP = WARP_MAT_N // MMA_N
33	BATCH_K = GROUP_K // MMA_K

36	ROW_STRIDE = MATMUL_K_VEC * 4

47	SHM_PAD_BYTES = 32  # kPaddingSizeA / kPaddingSizeB
48	SHM_PAD_U32 = SHM_PAD_BYTES // 4

302	    r_a: S.Tensor((M_TILES_PER_WARP, 4), S.u32),
303	    r_b: S.Tensor((N_TILES_PER_WARP, 4), S.u32),
304	    acc: S.Tensor(
305	        (M_TILES_PER_WARP * N_TILES_PER_WARP, 4),
306	        S.f32,
307	    ),
308	):
309	    for m in S.range(M_TILES_PER_WARP):
310	        r_a_bf16 = S.view(r_a[m], S.Tensor((2, 4), S.bf16))
311	        for n in S.range(N_TILES_PER_WARP):
312	            idx = m * N_TILES_PER_WARP + n
313	            r_b_bf16 = S.view(r_b[n], S.Tensor((2, 4), S.bf16))
314	            acc[idx] = S.amdgpu.mfma_f32_16x16x16_bf16(
315	                r_a_bf16[0], r_b_bf16[0], acc[idx]
316	            )
317	
318	    for m in S.range(M_TILES_PER_WARP):
319	        r_a_bf16 = S.view(r_a[m], S.Tensor((2, 4), S.bf16))
320	        for n in S.range(N_TILES_PER_WARP):
321	            idx = m * N_TILES_PER_WARP + n
322	            r_b_bf16 = S.view(r_b[n], S.Tensor((2, 4), S.bf16))
323	            acc[idx] = S.amdgpu.mfma_f32_16x16x16_bf16(
324	                r_a_bf16[1], r_b_bf16[1], acc[idx]
325	            )

328	@substrate.jit
329	def hot_loop_scheduler():
330	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 1, 0)
331	
332	    for _ in S.range(8):
333	        S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_DS_READ, 1, 0)
334	        S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 2, 0)
335	
336	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_S_BARRIER, 1, 0)
337	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 1, 0)
338	
339	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_DS_WRITE, 1, 0)
340	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 1, 0)
341	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_BUFFER_LOAD, 1, 0)
342	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 3, 0)
343	
344	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_DS_WRITE, 1, 0)
345	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 1, 0)
346	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_BUFFER_LOAD, 1, 0)
347	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 3, 0)
348	
349	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_DS_WRITE, 1, 0)
350	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 1, 0)
351	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_BUFFER_LOAD, 1, 0)
352	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 2, 0)
353	
354	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_DS_WRITE, 1, 0)
355	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 1, 0)
356	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_BUFFER_LOAD, 1, 0)
357	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 3, 0)
358	
359	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_DS_WRITE, 1, 0)
360	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 1, 0)
361	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_BUFFER_LOAD, 1, 0)
362	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 2, 0)
363	
364	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_DS_WRITE, 1, 0)
365	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 1, 0)
366	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_BUFFER_LOAD, 1, 0)
367	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 3, 0)
368	
369	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_DS_WRITE, 1, 0)
370	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 1, 0)
371	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_BUFFER_LOAD, 1, 0)
372	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 2, 0)
373	
374	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_DS_WRITE, 1, 0)
375	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 1, 0)
376	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_BUFFER_LOAD, 1, 0)
377	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 3, 0)
378	
379	    # barrier for hacky LLVM
380	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_S_BARRIER, 1, 0)
381	
382	    for _ in S.range(8):
383	        S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_DS_READ, 1, 0)
384	        S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 2, 0)

387	@substrate.jit
388	def write_results(
389	    id_m: S.u32,
390	    id_n: S.u32,
391	    acc: S.Tensor(
392	        (M_TILES_PER_WARP * N_TILES_PER_WARP, 4),
393	        S.f32,
394	    ),
395	    rsrc: S.Tensor((4,), S.u32),
396	    m: S.u32,
397	    n: S.u32,
398	):
399	    tid = S.thread_id(0)
400	    wid = tid // WARP_SIZE
401	    wtid = tid % WARP_SIZE
402	    wid_m = wid // WARP_PER_COL % WARP_PER_ROW
403	    wid_n = wid % WARP_PER_COL
404	    mma_k = wtid % MMA_K
405	    batch_k = wtid // MMA_K
406	
407	    warp_step_a = M_TILES_PER_WARP * TILE_M
408	    warp_step_b = N_TILES_PER_WARP * TILE_N
409	    large_step = SHM_PAD_ROWS * 16
410	
411	    output = S.make_local((4, N_TILES_PER_WARP), S.u16)
412	
413	    global_base = ((id_m * GROUP_M // VEC_SIZE) * n + id_n * GROUP_N // VEC_SIZE) * 16
414	
415	    for m_tile in S.range(M_TILES_PER_WARP):
416	        for n_tile in S.range(N_TILES_PER_WARP):
417	            t = S.make_local((4,), S.u16)
418	            v = S.view(t, S.Tensor((2,), S.u32))
419	            idx = m_tile * N_TILES_PER_WARP + n_tile
420	
421	            for l in S.range(2):
422	                lo = S.bitcast(acc[idx, l * 2 + 0], S.u32)
423	                hi = S.bitcast(acc[idx, l * 2 + 1], S.u32)
424	                tmp = S.amdgpu.perm(hi, lo, 0x07060302)
425	                v[l] = tmp
426	
427	            for i in S.range(4):
428	                output[i, n_tile] = t[i]
429	
430	        row_base = (
431	            wid_m * warp_step_a
432	            + batch_k * SHM_PAD_ROWS * 4
433	            + m_tile // SHM_PAD_ROWS * large_step
434	            + m_tile % SHM_PAD_ROWS
435	        )
436	        col_base = wid_n * warp_step_b + mma_k * SHM_PAD_ROWS
437	
438	        base = global_base + (row_base * n + col_base) * 2
439	
440	        output_packed = S.view(output, S.Tensor((4, 2), S.u32))
441	
442	        for i in S.range(4):
443	            row_offset = i * SHM_PAD_ROWS
444	            offset = (row_offset * n) * 2
445	            S.amdgpu.raw_buffer_store_x2(output_packed[i], rsrc, base, offset, 0)
446

448	@substrate.jit
449	def wgm_mapping(m: S.u32, n: S.u32) -> (S.u32, S.u32):
450	    linear_group_id = S.block_id(0)
451	    m_groups = m // GROUP_M
452	    n_groups = n // GROUP_N
453	
454	    total_groups = m_groups * n_groups
455	
456	    cu_count = S.convert(38 * 8, S.u32)
457	    wgm_xcc = S.convert(8, S.u32)
458	    workgroup_mapping = S.convert(32, S.u32)
459	
460	    linear_group_limit = (total_groups // wgm_xcc) * wgm_xcc
461	    cu_base = (linear_group_id // cu_count) * cu_count
462	    cu_xcc = (linear_group_id % cu_count) // wgm_xcc
463	    cu_base = cu_base + cu_xcc
464	
465	    cu_tail_limit = (total_groups // cu_count) * cu_count
466	    active_cu = (
467	        (total_groups % cu_count) if (linear_group_id > cu_tail_limit) else cu_count
468	    )
469	    cu_xcc_stride = (active_cu // wgm_xcc) * (linear_group_id % wgm_xcc)
470	    linear_group_mapped = cu_base + cu_xcc_stride
471	
472	    linear_group_id = (
473	        linear_group_mapped
474	        if (linear_group_id < linear_group_limit)
475	        else linear_group_id
476	    )
477	
478	    group_m = linear_group_id // n_groups
479	    group_n = linear_group_id - group_m * n_groups
480	
481	    mapping_block = group_m // workgroup_mapping
482	    mapping_linear = group_n + (group_m % workgroup_mapping) * n_groups
483	    mapping_groups = m_groups // workgroup_mapping
484	    mapping_tail = m_groups % workgroup_mapping
485	    mapping_tail = workgroup_mapping if (mapping_tail == 0) else mapping_tail
486	
487	    mapping_span = (
488	        mapping_tail if (mapping_block >= mapping_groups) else workgroup_mapping
489	    )
490	
491	    group_n = mapping_linear // mapping_span
492	    group_m = mapping_linear % mapping_span
493	    group_m = group_m + mapping_block * workgroup_mapping
494	
495	    return group_m, group_n
496

498	@substrate.jit
499	def _gemm_1stage_pipeline_kernel_batch2(
500	    A: S.Pointer(S.u32),
501	    B: S.Pointer(S.u32),
502	    C: S.Pointer(S.u32),
503	    m: S.u32,
504	    n: S.u32,
505	    k: S.u32,
506	):
507	    id_m, id_n = wgm_mapping(m, n)
508	    k_vec = k // VEC_SIZE
509	
510	    # Store A / B from registers to shared memory (padded layout).
511	    shm_a = S.make_shared((SHM_A_U32,), S.u32)
512	    shm_b = S.make_shared((SHM_B_U64,), S.u64)
513	    acc = S.make_local((GROUP_M * GROUP_N // THREADS // 4, 4,), S.f32)
514	    for i in S.range(GROUP_M * GROUP_N // THREADS // 4):
515	        for j in S.range(4):
516	            acc[i, j] = 0
517	
518	    acc = S.make_local((M_TILES_PER_WARP * N_TILES_PER_WARP, 4), S.f32)
519	    for i in S.range(M_TILES_PER_WARP * N_TILES_PER_WARP):
520	        for t in S.range(4):
521	            acc[i, t] = 0
522	
523	    rsrc_a = S.make_local((4,), S.u32)
524	    rsrc_b = S.make_local((4,), S.u32)
525	    rsrc_c = S.make_local((4,), S.u32)
526	
527	    layout_a = S.make_layout(
528	        (m, k_vec, 4),
529	        (k_vec * 4, 4, 1),
530	    )
531	    layout_b = S.make_layout(
532	        (n, k_vec, 4),
533	        (k_vec * 4, 4, 1),
534	    )
535	
536	    g_a = S.make_tensor(A, S.u32, layout_a)
537	    g_b = S.make_tensor(B, S.u32, layout_b)
538	    rsrc_a = S.amdgpu.make_rsrc(g_a, m * k * 2)
539	    rsrc_b = S.amdgpu.make_rsrc(g_b, n * k * 2)
540	
541	    layout_c = S.make_layout((m, n // 2), (n // 2, 1))
542	    g_c = S.make_tensor(C, S.u32, layout_c)
543	    rsrc_c = S.amdgpu.make_rsrc(g_c, m * n * 2)
544	
545	    k_total = k // GROUP_K
546	    k_total_u32 = S.convert(k_total, S.u32)
547	    k_stagger_mask = S.convert(0x7, S.u32)
548	    k_stagger_stride = S.convert(4, S.u32)
549	    stagger_data = id_n
550	    k_start = (stagger_data & k_stagger_mask) * k_stagger_stride
551	    k_start = k_start if (k_start < k_total_u32) else S.convert(0, S.u32)
552	
553	    # 2-stage single-buffer pipeline:
554	    # stage 0: prefetch next tile into registers
555	    # stage 1: compute current tile from shared memory
556	    reg_a = S.make_local((GROUP_M * GROUP_K // VEC_SIZE // THREADS, 4,), S.u32,)
557	    reg_b = S.make_local((B_LOADS_PER_THREAD, 4), S.u32)
558	    tile_a0 = S.make_local((1, 4), S.u32)
559	    tile_b0 = S.make_local((1, 4), S.u32)
560	    tile_a1 = S.make_local((1, 4), S.u32)
561	    tile_b1 = S.make_local((1, 4), S.u32)
562	
563	    # Prime the pipeline with staggered k_tile.
564	    load_global_a(A, m, k, id_m, k_start, reg_a)
565	    load_global_b_row_major(B, n, k, k_start, id_n, reg_b)
566	    store_shm_a(shm_a, reg_a)
567	    store_shm_b_transposed(shm_b, reg_b)
568	    S.syncthreads()
569	
570	    # Main pipeline loop: prefetch next tile while computing current
571	    for k_iter in S.range(k_tiles - 1):
572	        k_next = k_start + k_iter + 1
573	        k_next = k_next - (k_tiles_u32 if (k_next >= k_tiles_u32) else S.convert(0, S.u32))
574	        load_global_a(A, m, k, id_m, k_next, reg_a)
575	        load_global_b_row_major(B, n, k, k_next, id_n, reg_b)
576	
577	    load_shm_to_regs(shm, 0, tile_a0, tile_b0)
578	
579	    k_idx = S.convert(0, S.u32)
580	    while k_idx + 3 < k_total_u32:
581	        # Step 1
582	        k_idx = k_idx + 1
583	        load_shm_to_regs(shm, 1, tile_a1, tile_b1)
584	        matmul_from_regs(tile_a0, tile_b0, acc)
585	        S.syncthreads()
586	
587	        # Publish prefetched tile to shared memory for next iteration
588	        store_shm_a(shm_a, reg_a)
589	        store_shm_b_transposed(shm_b, reg_b)
590	        S.syncthreads()
591	
592	        load_shm_to_regs(shm, 0, tile_a0, tile_b0)
593	        matmul_from_regs(tile_a1, tile_b1, acc)
594	
595	        # Step 2
596	        k_idx = k_idx + 1
597	        load_shm_to_regs(shm, 1, tile_a1, tile_b1)
598	        matmul_from_regs(tile_a0, tile_b0, acc)
599	        S.syncthreads()
600	
601	        k_load = k_reg + 1
602	        k_load = k_load - k_total_u32 if (k_load >= k_total_u32) else k_load
603	        fetch_global_and_store_shm_pipeline(
604	            rsrc_a, rsrc_b, k, id_m, k_load, id_n, k_load, shm, reg_a, reg_b
605	        )
606	        k_reg = k_load
607	        S.syncthreads()
608	
609	        load_shm_to_regs(shm, 0, tile_a0, tile_b0)
610	        matmul_from_regs(tile_a1, tile_b1, acc)
611	
612	        hot_loop_scheduler()
613	        hot_loop_scheduler()
614	
615	    if k_idx + 2 < k_total_u32:
616	        k_idx = k_idx + 1
617	        load_shm_to_regs(shm, 1, tile_a1, tile_b1)
618	        matmul_from_regs(tile_a0, tile_b0, acc)
619	        S.syncthreads()
620	
621	        k_load = k_reg + 1
622	        k_load = k_load - k_total_u32 if (k_load >= k_total_u32) else k_load
623	        fetch_global_and_store_shm_pipeline(
624	            rsrc_a, rsrc_b, k, id_m, k_load, id_n, k_load, shm, reg_a, reg_b
625	        )
626	        k_reg = k_load
627	        S.syncthreads()
628	
629	        load_shm_to_regs(shm, 0, tile_a0, tile_b0)
630	        matmul_from_regs(tile_a1, tile_b1, acc)
631	
632	        hot_loop_scheduler()
633	
634	    if k_idx + 1 < k_total_u32:
635	        k_idx = k_idx + 1
636	        load_shm_to_regs(shm, 1, tile_a1, tile_b1)
637	        matmul_from_regs(tile_a0, tile_b0, acc)
638	        S.syncthreads()
639	
640	        store_shm(shm, reg_a, reg_b)
641	        S.syncthreads()
642	
643	        load_shm_to_regs(shm, 0, tile_a0, tile_b0)
644	        matmul_from_regs(tile_a1, tile_b1, acc)
645	
646	    if k_idx < k_total_u32:
647	        load_shm_to_regs(shm, 1, tile_a1, tile_b1)
648	        matmul_from_regs(tile_a0, tile_b0, acc)
649	        matmul_from_regs(tile_a1, tile_b1, acc)
650	
651	    write_results(id_m, id_n, acc, rsrc_c, m, n)
652

654	@substrate.jit
655	def _gemm_1stage_pipeline_transposed_b_kernel(
656	    A: S.Pointer(S.u32),
657	    B: S.Pointer(S.u32),
658	    C: S.Pointer(S.u32),
659	    m: S.u32,
660	    n: S.u32,
661	    k: S.u32,
662	):
663	    id_m, id_n = wgm_mapping(m, n)
664	
665	    shm_a = S.make_shared((SHM_A_U32,), S.u32)
666	    shm_b = S.make_shared((SHM_A_U32,), S.u32)
667	    acc = S.make_local((GROUP_M * GROUP_N // THREADS // 4, 4,), S.f32)
668	    for i in S.range(GROUP_M * GROUP_N // THREADS // 4):
669	        for j in S.range(4):
670	            acc[i, j] = 0
671	
672	    k_tiles = k // GROUP_K
673	    k_tiles_u32 = S.convert(k_tiles, S.u32)
674	    k_stagger_mask = S.convert(0x7, S.u32)
675	    k_stagger_stride = S.convert(4, S.u32)
676	    stagger_data = id_n
677	    k_start = (stagger_data & k_stagger_mask) * k_stagger_stride
678	    k_start = k_start if (k_start < k_tiles_u32) else S.convert(0, S.u32)
679	
680	    reg_a = S.make_local((GROUP_M * GROUP_K // VEC_SIZE // THREADS, 4,), S.u32,)
681	    reg_b = S.make_local((GROUP_N * GROUP_K // VEC_SIZE // THREADS, 4,), S.u32,)
682	    tile_a0 = S.make_local((1, 4), S.u32)
683	    tile_b0 = S.make_local((1, 4), S.u32)
684	    tile_a1 = S.make_local((1, 4), S.u32)
685	    tile_b1 = S.make_local((1, 4), S.u32)
686	
687	    load_global_a(A, m, k, id_m, k_start, reg_a)
688	    load_global_b_transposed(B, n, k, id_n, k_start, reg_b)
689	    store_shm_a(shm_a, reg_a)
690	    store_shm_b_plain(shm_b, reg_b)
691	    S.syncthreads()
692	
693	    for k_iter in S.range(k_tiles - 1):
694	        k_next = k_start + k_iter + 1
695	        k_next = k_next - (k_tiles_u32 if (k_next >= k_tiles_u32) else S.convert(0, S.u32))
696	        load_global_a(A, m, k, id_m, k_next, reg_a)
697	        load_global_b_transposed(B, n, k, id_n, k_next, reg_b)
698	
699	        for i in S.range(M_TILES_PER_WARP):
700	            for j in S.range(N_TILES_PER_WARP):
701	                acc_idx = i * N_TILES_PER_WARP + j
702	                load_shm_to_regs_transposed_b(shm_a, shm_b, i, j, 0, tile_a0, tile_b0)
703	                load_shm_to_regs_transposed_b(shm_a, shm_b, i, j, 1, tile_a1, tile_b1)
704	                matmul_from_regs(tile_a0, tile_b0, acc, acc_idx)
705	                matmul_from_regs(tile_a1, tile_b1, acc, acc_idx)
706	        S.syncthreads()
707	
708	        store_shm_a(shm_a, reg_a)
709	        store_shm_b_plain(shm_b, reg_b)
710	        S.syncthreads()
711	
712	    for i in S.range(M_TILES_PER_WARP):
713	        for j in S.range(N_TILES_PER_WARP):
714	            acc_idx = i * N_TILES_PER_WARP + j
715	            load_shm_to_regs_transposed_b(shm_a, shm_b, i, j, 0, tile_a0, tile_b0)
716	            load_shm_to_regs_transposed_b(shm_a, shm_b, i, j, 1, tile_a1, tile_b1)
717	            matmul_from_regs(tile_a0, tile_b0, acc, acc_idx)
718	            matmul_from_regs(tile_a1, tile_b1, acc, acc_idx)
719	
720	    write_results(id_m, id_n, acc, C, m, n)
721

723	def _validate_gemm_inputs(
724	    A: torch.Tensor,
725	    B: torch.Tensor,
726	):
727	    if not isinstance(A, torch.Tensor) or not isinstance(B, torch.Tensor):
728	        raise TypeError("A and B must be torch.Tensor")
729	    if A.ndim != 2 or B.ndim != 2:
730	        raise ValueError(
731	            f"A and B must be rank-2 tensors (got A.ndim={A.ndim}, B.ndim={B.ndim})"
732	        )
733	    if A.dtype != torch.bfloat16 or B.dtype != torch.bfloat16:
734	        raise TypeError(
735	            f"A and B must be torch.bfloat16 (got A={A.dtype}, B={B.dtype})"
736	        )
737	    if A.device.type != "cuda" or B.device.type != "cuda":
738	        raise ValueError(
739	            f"A and B must be CUDA tensors (got A={A.device}, B={B.device})"
740	        )
741	    if A.device != B.device:
742	        raise ValueError(
743	            f"A and B must be on the same device (got {A.device} and {B.device})"
744	        )
745	
746	def _prepare_out(
747	    m: int,
748	    n: int,
749	    device: torch.device,
750	    out: torch.Tensor | None,
751	) -> torch.Tensor:
752	    if out is not None and not isinstance(out, torch.Tensor):
753	        raise TypeError("out must be torch.Tensor")
754	    if out is None or out.numel() == 0:
755	        out = torch.empty((m, n), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device=device)
756	    elif out.ndim != 2 or out.shape != (m, n):
757	        raise ValueError(f"out must have shape {(m, n)} (got {tuple(out.shape)})")
758	    elif out.dtype != torch.bfloat16:
759	        raise TypeError(f"out must be torch.bfloat16 (got {out.dtype})")
760	    elif out.device != device:
761	        raise ValueError(f"out must be on {device} (got {out.device})")
762	
763	    return out
764	
765	
766	def gemm_1stage_pipeline_row_major(
767	    A: torch.Tensor,
768	    B: torch.Tensor,
769	    out: torch.Tensor | None = None,
770	) -> torch.Tensor:
771	    _validate_gemm_inputs(A, B)
772	    m, k = A.shape
773	    b_k, n = B.shape
774	
775	    if k != b_k:
776	        raise ValueError(
777	            "K dimension mismatch: B must have standard row-major GEMM shape (K, N) "
778	            f"(got A.shape={A.shape}, B.shape={B.shape})"
779	        )
780	
781	    gemm_1stage_validate_shape(m, n, k)
782	    out = _prepare_out(m, n, A.device, out)
783	
784	    grid, block = gemm_launch_config(m, n)
785	    _gemm_1stage_pipeline_kernel_batch2[lambda: (grid, block)](A, B, out, m, n, k)
786	    return out
787	
788	
789	def gemm_1stage_pipeline_transposed_b(
790	    A: torch.Tensor,
791	    B_t: torch.Tensor,
792	    out: torch.Tensor | None = None,
793	) -> torch.Tensor:
794	    _validate_gemm_inputs(A, B_t)
795	    m, k = A.shape
796	    n, b_k = B_t.shape
797	
798	    if k != b_k:
799	        raise ValueError(
800	            "K dimension mismatch: transposed B must have shape (N, K) "
801	            f"(got A.shape={A.shape}, B.shape={B_t.shape})"
802	        )
803	
804	    gemm_1stage_validate_shape(m, n, k)
805	    out = _prepare_out(m, n, A.device, out)
806	
807	    grid, block = gemm_1stage_launch_config(m, n)
808	    _gemm_1stage_pipeline_transposed_b_kernel[lambda: (grid, block)](A, B_t, out, m, n, k)
809	    return out
810	
811	
812	def gemm_1stage_pipeline(
813	    A: torch.Tensor,
814	    B: torch.Tensor,
815	    out: torch.Tensor | None = None,
816	) -> torch.Tensor:
817	    return gemm_1stage_pipeline_row_major(A, B, out)
818	
819	
820	__all__ = [
821	    "gemm_1stage_pipeline",
822	    "gemm_1stage_pipeline_row_major",
823	    "gemm_1stage_pipeline_transposed_b",
824	]
